page.title: Caravan & Trade
page.description: In Quinfall a caravan isn't just hauling — it's the spine of the economy and a PvP battlefield. Rent an animal at the Caravan Vendor, buy a package, pick the right route. PvE channel is safe, PvP channel pays +20%.

hero.title: 12 cities, 7 harbours, +20% PvP bonus.
hero.body: Rent a donkey, ox, camel or elephant from the Caravan Vendor NPC, grab a package and run it to the next city or harbour. PvE channel is safe but pays no bonus; PvP channel allows attacks and pays +20%. As of patch v1.0.0.6 an unattended caravan stays put for 1 hour — your package isn't lost.

flow.title: How a Caravan Run Works
flow.subtitle: The standard five-step loop — every Caravan Vendor in every city follows it.

rent.title: Rent an animal
rent.body: Pick donkey, ox, camel or elephant from the Caravan Vendor. The mount is summoned; the rental clock starts.

package.title: Buy the package
package.body: Buy the city's package from the same NPC. Package size scales with the rented animal's cargo.

route.title: Set the route
route.body: Pick a destination city or harbour. Distance + channel type (PvE/PvP) decide the payout.

travel.title: Travel
travel.body: Stay active: in a PvP channel other players can attack. If you log out, the animal stays put for 1 hour.

deliver.title: Deliver
deliver.body: Hand the package to the destination NPC; payout hits the wallet immediately. The animal can be returned within the rental window.

animals.title: Rental Types
animals.subtitle: Standard Caravan Vendor inventory: 4 animals, with cargo and rent climbing as the tier rises. Premium mounts unlock through V-Coin / events.

standard.title: Standard Rentals
standard.body: The four base options at every Caravan Vendor. The first three are cheap; the elephant is a real investment.

premium.title: Premium Caravan Skins
premium.body: Top-tier looks unlocked via the shop or events. No stat advantage, purely cosmetic.

donkey.name: Donkey
donkey.body: The cheapest option. Slow but carries cargo; ideal for starter routes.

ox.name: Ox
ox.body: Slower than the donkey but among the highest cargo capacity. Steady pace, long routes.

camel.name: Camel
camel.body: Immune to the heat debuff in the desert. The non-negotiable mount on Horus routes.

elephant.name: Elephant
elephant.body: Top HP and cargo. The howdah lets you fight from the back; ideal for guild caravans.

desertCaravan.name: Desert Caravan
desertCaravan.body: Premium look themed for the Horus route.

channels.title: PvE Safe, PvP +20%
channels.subtitle: The critical mechanic updated in patch v1.0.0.6 (Feb 14, 2026). The bonus argument now lives entirely in the channel choice.

pve.title: PvE Channel
pve.body: Other players can't attack your caravan. No risk, no loss — but the previous 5% bonus was removed entirely.

pvp.title: PvP Channel
pvp.body: Attacks are open. Get caught and you drop the package or lose it. This is where real traders earn — bonus raised to +20%.

unattended.title: v1.0.0.6: Unattended Protection
unattended.body: If you log out or the server restarts, the caravan animal stays put for 1 hour. The package isn't lost and the caravan doesn't unwind during that window.

cities.title: Caravan Route Hubs
cities.subtitle: The 12 major cities on the world map — grouped by region. Each hosts a Caravan Vendor; routes are drawn between these.

note.title: Distance = Payout
note.body: Short routes between adjacent cities are quick but pay less; long routes (e.g. desert ↔ west) pay more. Combine the distance with the channel choice — a long PvP-channel route compounds the +20% bonus, but the risk scales too.

sea.title: Harbours and the Harbor Master
sea.subtitle: The parallel system to land caravans. Pick up a package at a Harbor Master and ship it to another harbour. Key rule: payout depends on the departure harbour, not the destination.

harborTable.label: PAYOUT TABLE
harborTable.title: Estimated Payout per Harbor
harborTable.note: Based on community observation, by departure harbour. Traystable pays the most; Edgeshore and Silvercrest the least.

harborMaster.title: Harbor Master at every harbour
harborMaster.body: One NPC handles ship management, caravan packages and fishing services — all in one.

departurePayout.title: Departure harbour decides pay
departurePayout.body: Payout depends on where you picked up the package, not where you drop it. Pricey departure = higher gold.

destroyOnDock.title: Packages destroyed on dock
destroyOnDock.body: Docking the ship can destroy the package on board — be careful until you reach the destination NPC.

pirate.title: Pirate risk in PvP channel
pirate.body: On a PvP channel other players can attack your ship. Same logic as land caravans: risk × reward.

channelSwitch.title: Channel switch
channelSwitch.body: Switching channels requires summoning the ship to a harbour first; no mid-ocean swap.

strategy.title: Which Path for Which Profile?
strategy.subtitle: Safe income for new players, scale for the mid-game, risk-and-reward for the PvPer, ops for the guild.

starter.title: 🌱 New Player
bullets: First route: a short hop between two adjacent cities, donkey, PvE channel — zero risk, learn the loop.
Find the Caravan Vendor in every major city.
If you have to log out, no panic — 1-hour unattended protection covers you.
Move from low-payout starter loops to long routes like Horus / Pabas.

midGame.title: 💰 Mid-Game Yield
bullets: Rent a camel — the no-water-debuff edge changes everything around Horus.
Set up a circular multi-city loop (Pabas → Reasya → Horus → Pabas).
Even on PvE channel, distance × package size delivers serious gold.
If a harbour is on the way, chain land + sea caravans.

pvpHigh.title: ⚔ PvP Risk Stacking
bullets: PvP channel: +20% bonus stacks meaningfully on long routes.
Don't go solo: bring at least one guard player.
Elephant + armoured escort buys the package real time.
Logging out under attack = animal stays 1 hour, but the package is at risk.

guildOps.title: 🏰 Guild Operation
bullets: Elephant + premium skins (Desert Caravan, Sultan's Guardian) are guild investments.
Run multiple caravans on one route: 1 guard for 4 caravans — economy of scale.
Run sea caravans in parallel: Traystable departures (~1,500g) are the highest payout.
Plan channel choice around the guild calendar — primetime PvP channels get crowded.
